title: viola montana
intro: Viola montana, also known as the alpine pansy or alpine violet, is a perennial plant species belonging to the viola genus. It is native to mountainous regions in Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, and is known for its small, delicate purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Viola montana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
